methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $106M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
ARX Robotics is a Series A–stage defense and security robotics company founded by Marc Wietfeld that has raised $106M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of 10 private peers and 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) operating in the same sector, including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses the $106M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, yielding $212M. The peer-median method examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M). The median of this sample is $300M, with a range spanning $150M to $500M.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $180M (low) to $345M (high), with a median estimate of $256M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables support the upper range. Key uncertainties include ARX's relative competitive positioning within the peer set, technology differentiation relative to Anduril and other private incumbents, and near-term revenue trajectory relative to capital deployed. The $76M spread between low and high estimates reflects material dispersion in peer valuations and limited transparency into ARX's specific operational metrics.
